Basic Science & Tech.q: Which of the following safety signs indicates a mandatory action that must be taken in the workshop?
The process of giving immediate care and support to an injured person before professional medical help arrives is ________.
Which of the following is a major cause of workshop accidents?
Wood obtained from coniferous trees that possess needle-like leaves is generally classified as ________.
The process of reducing the moisture content of timber to make it suitable for engineering construction is called ________.
Which of the following defects in timber is caused by natural growth forces rather than poor seasoning?
Metals that do not contain any iron elements in their composition are known as ________.
An alloy of copper and zinc fused together to create a durable workshop material is called ________.
The property of a metal that allows it to be hammered or rolled into thin sheets without breaking is ________.
Plastics that can be softened by heating and remolded into new shapes repeatedly are classified as ________.
The chemical process of heating raw rubber with sulfur to improve its strength and elasticity is called ________.
Which of the following drawing instruments is specifically used for drawing horizontal straight lines?
A set square combination can be utilized by a draftsman to draw angles at intervals of ________ degrees.
The type of line used in technical drawing to show hidden details or outlines of an object is the ________.
An angle that measures exactly more than 90 degrees but less than 180 degrees is classified as a/an ________ angle.
A triangle that has all three of its sides unequal in length profile is called a ________ triangle.
A polygon with exactly six straight structural boundary lines is known as a ________.
A straight line drawn from the center of a circle to touch any point on its outer circumference is the ________.
In orthographic projection, the view obtained when looking directly down at the object from above is the ________.
The angle of projection where the object view is positioned between the observer and the plane of projection is ________.
Which type of drawing view showcases an object in a three-dimensional layout with one face parallel to the drawing plane?
The standard metric drawing sheet designated as 'A4' measures exactly ________ mm.
Which woodcarving tool is specifically designed for cutting mortise joints or deep structural holes in timber?
The hand tool used to smooth and flatten the surface of a wooden board during processing is the ________.
Which of the following saw types is optimized for cutting intricate curves in thin plywood templates?
The workshop driving tool used to hit the wooden handle of a chisel safely without causing cracks is a ________.
Which of the following metalworking tools is used for marking circular lines on sheet metals?
The hand tool used to cut metal rods, bars, and sheets in the workshop is a ________.
Which tool is applied to verify if a wooden surface is perfectly square or at a right angle (90°) context?
The operational process of making a clean hole in a metal plate using a drilling machine is ________.
Which component of a building structure forms the base barrier constructed below ground level to distribute weight evenly?
The horizontal structural concrete beam cast across window and door openings to support walls above is a ________.
A mixture of cement, sand, gravel, and water combined together forms a durable building matrix called ________.
Which type of drive system transmits power between two parallel shafts separated by a considerable distance using pulleys?
To prevent a belt drive from slipping under a heavy load, workshop technicians apply ________ to the belt interface.
When two gears mesh directly together, the driven gear always rotates in a direction ________ to the driving gear.
Which gear configuration is specifically applied to convert rotational motion into linear motion layouts?
The mechanical component used to connect two rotating shafts together to transmit power safely is a ________.
Which mechanism is used in motor vehicles to temporarily disconnect engine power from the transmission system during gear changes?
The primary function of a mechanical brake assembly on any moving vehicle system is to ________.
Which type of maintenance culture involves repairing a machine tool only after it fails completely?
Applying grease or lubricants to the moving parts of workshop machines primarily reduces ________.
Which of the following is a vital non-renewable energy source resource utilized globally?
An electronic component that allows electric current to flow through it in only one direction is a/an ________.
The electrical instrument used to measure the rate of electric current flow in a circuit loop is the ________.
Which electronic component is primarily used to store electric charges within an operating circuit unit?
The process of joining two metal pipes or wires together using a molten filler metal at low temperature is ________.
Which of the following materials acts as an excellent electrical insulator?
The system that utilizes compressed air to transmit power and control mechanisms is called a ________ system.
A hydraulic system relies on the transmission of pressure through a/an ________ medium to execute mechanical work.
Which of the following plumbing fittings is used to close or terminate the dead end of a pipeline safely?
The tool used by plumbers to cut internal or external threads on a metal pipe frame is a ________.
A simple device made of a coiled metal line that returns to its original shape after being compressed or stretched is a ________.
Which machine tool is widely referred to as the 'mother of all machine shops' because it shapes materials by rotating them against a cutter?
The primary function of walls in a building construction project includes all the following EXCEPT ________.
o: Supporting the weight of the roof structure

Basic Science & Tech.q: Which type of drawing view provides a split-open look at an internal layout as if the object was cut through with a saw blade?
The property of a material that enables it to stretch under structural strain and return completely to its original dimensions when released is ________.
Which structural member forms the inclined timber frame pieces that support the roofing sheets directly?
The system of transport that moves heavy quantities of fluid assets or gas across long geographical expanses is ________ transport.
An protective item that must be worn to safeguard the eyes when executing electric arc welding operations is ________.
